steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: For the two-line system to have no solution, line $k$ must be parallel to line $h$ but must not be the same line.

steps.title: Find the intercept match
steps.content:
From the graph, line $h$ crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,-8)$. For line $k$, substitute $x=0$ into $sx+40y=t$.
$$40y=t$$

steps.title: Reject the identical line
steps.content: If $t=-320$, then $40y=-320$, so $y=-8$. With the required parallel slope, that would make line $k$ identical to line $h$, giving infinitely many solutions instead of no solution. The answer is D.
